dom: God of the sun and creation
fa: Believed to travel across the sky by day and through the underworld by night in a solar barque

dom: God of the afterlife, resurrection and fertility
fa: Murdered by his brother Set and later resurrected by Isis, becoming ruler of the underworld

dom: Goddess of magic, healing and motherhood
fa: Reassembled her husband Osiris's body after his murder using powerful magic

dom: God of the sky and kingship
fa: Egyptian pharaohs were believed to be his living embodiment on Earth

dom: God of chaos, storms and the desert
fa: Murdered his brother Osiris out of jealousy, becoming a symbol of chaos and disorder

dom: God of mummification and the afterlife
fa: Believed to guide the weighing of souls' hearts against the feather of truth

dom: God of wisdom, writing and the moon
fa: Credited with inventing writing and serving as scribe of the gods

dom: Goddess of love, joy and motherhood
fa: Worshipped as a protector of women and often depicted nursing the pharaoh

dom: Lioness goddess of war and healing
fa: Said to have once nearly destroyed humanity in a fit of rage before being tricked into stopping

dom: Cat goddess of protection and the home
fa: Cats were considered sacred in Egypt largely due to her association with them

dom: Creator god and patron of craftsmen
fa: Believed by some Egyptian traditions to have created the world simply by speaking it into existence

dom: King of the gods, later merged with Ra as Amun-Ra
fa: Rose from a local Theban god to become the chief deity of the Egyptian pantheon

dom: Goddess of the sky
sym: Star-covered body arched over the earth
fa: Depicted as a star-covered woman arching over the earth god Geb, swallowing the sun each evening

dom: God of the earth
fa: Believed his laughter caused earthquakes

dom: God of air and light
fa: Believed to hold up the sky goddess Nut, separating her from the earth god Geb

dom: Goddess of moisture and rain
fa: Twin sister of Shu, together representing the earliest pair of gods created by Atum

dom: Goddess of mourning and protection of the dead
fa: Helped her sister Isis search for and mourn the body of the slain Osiris

dom: Crocodile god of the Nile and fertility
fa: Worshipped near the Nile as a protector against crocodile attacks, ironically embodied by one

dom: Ram-headed god of creation
fa: Believed to have molded humans and their souls out of clay on a potter's wheel

dom: Goddess of truth, justice and cosmic order
fa: A person's heart was weighed against her feather after death to judge their worthiness

dom: God of the lotus flower, beauty and healing
fa: Believed to have emerged from a lotus blossom at the dawn of creation

dom: Dwarf god protector of households and childbirth
fa: Unlike most Egyptian gods, he was usually shown facing forward rather than in profile

dom: Hippopotamus goddess protector of childbirth
fa: Combined features of a hippo, lion and crocodile to ward off evil during pregnancy

dom: Cobra goddess protector of Lower Egypt
fa: Depicted on the pharaoh's crown as a protective cobra ready to strike enemies

dom: Vulture goddess protector of Upper Egypt
fa: Paired with Wadjet to symbolize the unification of Upper and Lower Egypt

dom: Scorpion goddess of protection and healing
fa: Believed to protect against venomous stings and bites despite her scorpion form

dom: Goddess of war, hunting and weaving
fa: One of the oldest deities in the Egyptian pantheon, sometimes credited as a creator goddess

dom: Mother goddess, wife of Amun
fa: Her name means 'mother' in ancient Egyptian, reflecting her maternal role in the pantheon

dom: God of the moon
fa: Believed to govern time and the passing of months through the lunar cycle

dom: God of the sun disk
sym: Solar disk with rays ending in hands
fa: Elevated by Pharaoh Akhenaten as the sole god in one of history's earliest monotheistic movements

dom: Serpent deity of chaos and darkness
fa: Believed to attack the sun god Ra's boat every night, requiring the other gods to fend him off

dom: Primordial creator god
fa: Believed to have created the first gods by either spitting or masturbating them into existence

dom: Personification of the primordial waters of chaos
fa: Represented the formless waters that existed before creation, from which Atum emerged

dom: God of magic and medicine
fa: Personified the very concept of magic itself, believed to predate the gods

dom: Cobra goddess of the harvest and nourishment
fa: Worshipped at harvest time to ensure a plentiful and safe grain supply

dom: Falcon god of war guarding the eastern frontier
fa: Served as a protector deity guarding Egypt's eastern border against invaders

dom: God of fertility, the desert and male virility
fa: One of the oldest Egyptian gods, worshipped since predynastic times

dom: Goddess of writing, measurement and record-keeping
fa: Credited with recording the reigns of pharaohs and measuring the foundations of temples

dom: God of war and hunting
fa: His name means 'sky-bearer', reflecting his role as a warrior who upholds cosmic order

dom: Jackal god known as the 'opener of ways'
fa: Believed to lead the pharaoh and the dead safely on their journeys

dom: God of the annual Nile flood
sym: Papyrus and lotus, blue or green skin
fa: Worshipped for bringing the fertile floodwaters that sustained Egyptian agriculture

dom: Cobra goddess protector of the Valley of the Kings
fa: Believed to punish tomb robbers with snake bites or blindness

dom: Devourer of the unworthy dead
sym: Crocodile head, lion body, hippo legs
fa: Waited beside the scales of judgment to devour the hearts of those found unworthy

dom: One of the Four Sons of Horus, protector of the liver
fa: Guarded the liver of the deceased under the protection of the goddess Isis

dom: One of the Four Sons of Horus, protector of the lungs
fa: Guarded the lungs of the deceased under the protection of the goddess Nephthys

dom: One of the Four Sons of Horus, protector of the stomach
fa: Guarded the stomach of the deceased under the protection of the goddess Neith

dom: One of the Four Sons of Horus, protector of the intestines
fa: Guarded the intestines of the deceased under the protection of the goddess Serqet

dom: The underworld realm of the dead
fa: Believed to be divided into twelve regions the sun god Ra passed through each night

dom: God personifying fate and destiny
fa: Believed to determine the length and course of a person's life at their birth

dom: Goddess of childbirth and destiny
fa: Believed to be present at every birth to help determine the child's fate

dom: Personification of perception and understanding
fa: Often paired with Hu (authority) as companions who accompanied Ra

dom: Personification of authoritative, creative speech
fa: Represented the divine utterance believed to have brought creation into being

dom: Goddess of the Nile's cataracts and fertility
fa: Believed to guard Egypt's southern border and purify the pharaoh with Nile water

dom: Goddess of the Nile River and its cataracts
fa: Worshipped especially in the region around the Nile's first cataract

dom: Falcon-headed god of the necropolis
fa: Closely associated with craftsmen and the funerary practices of the Memphis region

dom: Personification of the Mediterranean Sea or great marshes
fa: Represented the fertile marshlands and sea that bordered ancient Egypt

dom: Syncretic god combining Osiris and the Apis bull
fa: Deliberately created under the Ptolemaic dynasty to blend Egyptian and Greek religious traditions

dom: Sacred bull deity linked to Ptah and later Osiris
fa: A living bull was selected by priests and worshipped as a physical incarnation of the god

dom: Bull-associated god from Middle Egyptian mythology
fa: Central figure in the ancient 'Tale of Two Brothers', an early example of Egyptian mythological storytelling

dom: Alternate rendering of the god of the lotus and healing
fa: Sometimes depicted as a young child seated on a lotus flower

dom: Lioness goddess of the desert margins
fa: Worshipped as a fierce huntress goddess in the region of Beni Hasan

dom: Lioness warrior goddess
fa: Her name means 'she who massacres', reflecting her role as a war deity

dom: Early god personifying the moon
fa: An older lunar deity later largely absorbed into the worship of Khonsu and Thoth

dom: Serpent god associated with protection and the afterlife
fa: Believed to bind together a person's spiritual forces after death
